data.body: R2 (b080) GROUNDING CORRECTION (2026-07-17): the epic Context/ordering says 'R2/R3/R4 each touch criteria_routing.json and land serially in any order (file-impact keeps them out of one batch)'. R2's implementation grounding REFUTES this for R2: the operator-attested evidence-kind lint is a P6-family static DET floor check (extends p6_ac_quality in det_floor.py). DET floor checks (P1-P9) are NOT routed through criteria_routing.json — registry.validate_packaged_routing() (registry.py:445) flags any routing key not in CANONICAL_LLM as an ORPHAN and fails CI, and det_invariants only consumes .rebar/-overlay exec:DET criteria backed by CODE grounding detectors (engine_b), which is structurally wrong for an AC-TEXT lexicon. So R2 does NOT touch criteria_routing.json (its file_impact is det_floor.py + workflow_ops.py + tests + docs/experiments artifacts + docs/plan-review-gate.md). The serialization-on-criteria_routing.json rationale still holds for R3/R4, but not for R2 — R2 has no criteria_routing.json file-conflict with them. (Flagged as plan-review G7 advisory on b080.)
